title: Dubai reveals 48-hour visa application process - Arabian Business: Latest News on the Middle East, Real
summary: The General Directorate of Identity and Foreigner Affairs in Dubai has issued a reminder that those applying for either a 30 or 60-day tourist visa will have their application processed within 48 hours.
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
Dubai is offering single-entry tourist visas valid for 30 or 60 days with processing times estimated at 48 working hours, as the emirate attempts to streamline entry procedures for visitors.

The General Directorate of Residency and Foreigners Affairs in Dubai (GDRFA) issued a reminder on its Instagram page as they continue to proactively highlight the ease and speed at which tourist visas can now be processed.

The process is straightforward and simple, and has been designed to provide a seamless entry experience to Dubai.

All applicants need to do is have a personal photo and a valid passport, or National ID card, which is dependent on certain countries.

The applications are then submitted through authorised tourism offices and the service will be completed within 48 hours.

The Instagram post by the GDFRA was entitled “easy steps” – and declared that Dubai welcomed its visitors and was committed to delivering frictionless experiences regarding entry.

Dubai is one of the world’s most popular tourist and business destinations, but is always looking at ways it can improve experiences for citizens, residents and tourists.

What types of Dubai tourist visas are available?

30-day single-entry tourist visa:

  • Valid for one entry into the UAE.
  • Stay permitted for up to 30 days.
  • Usually extendable from within the UAE for an additional fee.

60-day single-entry tourist visa:

  • Allows a longer stay of up to 60 days
  • Often used by longer-term visitors, remote workers, or people exploring relocation opportunities.

Processing times:

Typical processing time is within 48 hours after submission. However, the GDFRA has warned that delays can occur especially during weekends, peak holiday times and UAE public holidays.

How do you apply?

Applications are usually made through UAE carriers such as Emirates, Etihad Airways or flydubai and licensed travel agencies across the country.

Applications can also be made through UAE hotel partners and visa portals.

How much does it cost?

Fees vary depending on a range of factors that include; nationality, processing speed, whether extensions are included and service provider charges, but typically a 30-day visa will range between $90-$150, whilst a 60-day visa will set you back between $150-$250.

Last week, the GDRFA announced a “smart medical visa” designed to serve patients travelling to the emirate for treatment.
